abstract: Returns a list of requests to install apps that exceed the maximum allowed age rating for the device.

declaration.code: nonisolated final func currentAgeExceptionRequests() async throws -> [AppLibrary.ExceptionRequest]

overview:
## Discussion

When a person attempts to install an app with an age rating that exceeds the maximum allowed for the device, the framework presents a sheet that enables the person to request an exception from a parent or guardian. When the person proceeds with the request, the framework adds an instance of [doc://com.apple.MarketplaceKit/documentation/MarketplaceKit/AppLibrary/ExceptionRequest] for the app to this list.

The parent or guardian reviews the request in Messages on their device, or in person on the originating device, by responding to a system-provided sheet. After the parent or guardian makes a decision, the framework removes the app’s [doc://com.apple.MarketplaceKit/documentation/MarketplaceKit/AppLibrary/ExceptionRequest] from this list.

Keep the person informed of the [doc://com.apple.MarketplaceKit/documentation/MarketplaceKit/AppLibrary/ExceptionRequest/status-swift.property] of the current exception requests. For example, if the exception status is [doc://com.apple.MarketplaceKit/documentation/MarketplaceKit/AppLibrary/ExceptionRequest/Status-swift.enum/pending], you can show a Pending label in place of an Install button for the associated app.

For more information, see [doc://com.apple.MarketplaceKit/documentation/marketplacekit/providing-age-rating-appropriate-content].

# currentAgeExceptionRequests()

**Instance Method**

Returns a list of requests to install apps that exceed the maximum allowed age rating for the device.

## Declaration

```swift
nonisolated final func currentAgeExceptionRequests() async throws -> [AppLibrary.ExceptionRequest]
```

## Discussion

When a person attempts to install an app with an age rating that exceeds the maximum allowed for the device, the framework presents a sheet that enables the person to request an exception from a parent or guardian. When the person proceeds with the request, the framework adds an instance of [doc://com.apple.MarketplaceKit/documentation/MarketplaceKit/AppLibrary/ExceptionRequest] for the app to this list.

The parent or guardian reviews the request in Messages on their device, or in person on the originating device, by responding to a system-provided sheet. After the parent or guardian makes a decision, the framework removes the app’s [doc://com.apple.MarketplaceKit/documentation/MarketplaceKit/AppLibrary/ExceptionRequest] from this list.

Keep the person informed of the [doc://com.apple.MarketplaceKit/documentation/MarketplaceKit/AppLibrary/ExceptionRequest/status-swift.property] of the current exception requests. For example, if the exception status is [doc://com.apple.MarketplaceKit/documentation/MarketplaceKit/AppLibrary/ExceptionRequest/Status-swift.enum/pending], you can show a Pending label in place of an Install button for the associated app.

For more information, see [doc://com.apple.MarketplaceKit/documentation/marketplacekit/providing-age-rating-appropriate-content].

## Checking for age-rating based content restrictions

- **maximumAllowedAgeRating**: An age rating that specifies the maximum rating set for content on the device.
- **AppLibrary.ExceptionRequest**: A structure that describes an app that a person requests permission to install.
